title: Corrected Age Calculator for Premature Babies | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Calculate Your Premature Baby's Corrected Age
description: Calculate your premature baby's corrected age in seconds. Enter birth date and gestational weeks to find adjusted age for development & growth tracking. Free.
intro: **Premature babies** (born before week 37 of pregnancy) need their development evaluated using **corrected age**, not chronological age. Corrected age 'subtracts' the weeks that were missing to reach full term (40 weeks). This is essential for fairly assessing developmental milestones, weight, height, and feeding.
keyTakeaway: **Corrected age = chronological age − weeks of prematurity**. Used up to **2–3 years** for development and growth tracking. A baby born at 32 weeks has 8 weeks of 'adjustment'.
useCases: Your baby was born prematurely and you need to calculate their corrected age.
Your pediatrician uses corrected age and you want to understand the calculation.
You want to compare your premature baby's development against standard milestones.
You need corrected age to interpret growth percentile charts.
You want to know when to stop using corrected age and switch to chronological age.

example.title: Example: born January 1, 2026 at 32 weeks
steps: **Chronological age** (as of April 16): 3 months and 15 days.
example.result: Chronological age: **3 months 15 days**. Corrected age: **~1 month 19 days**. For development and growth evaluation, use the corrected age.

explanation:
## What Is Corrected Age?

```
Corrected age = Chronological age − (40 − gestational weeks at birth)
```

For example, a baby born at 30 weeks has 10 weeks of prematurity. If they're currently 6 months old chronologically, their corrected age is approximately 4 months.

## What Is Corrected Age Used For?

| Area | Corrected age used until |
|---|---|
| Motor development | 2–3 years |
| Growth curves (weight/height) | 2 years |
| Complementary feeding | Corrected age is used |
| Vaccinations | Chronological age is used |

## Prematurity Classification

| Weeks at birth | Classification |
|---|---|
| < 28 | Extremely premature |
| 28–31 | Very premature |
| 32–33 | Moderately premature |
| 34–36 | Late premature |

## Related Calculators

- [Motor Development](/infant-motor-development-milestones) — developmental milestones by corrected age.
- [Baby Percentile](/baby-growth-percentile-chart) — use corrected age for accurate tracking.

faq.q: When do I stop using corrected age?
faq.a: Used until age 2–3 years for development and growth tracking. After that, the difference becomes negligible and you'll use chronological age. Very premature infants (born before 28 weeks) may need corrected age longer.

faq.q: What age do I use for my baby's vaccinations?
faq.a: Vaccinations are given by **chronological age** (from birth date), not corrected age. This is because premature babies need protection as soon as possible.

faq.q: When should I start solid foods for my premature baby?
faq.a: Generally at **6 months corrected age** (not chronological), as long as your baby shows signs of readiness (sitting up, loss of tongue-thrust reflex). Always consult your pediatrician.

faq.q: Will my premature baby catch up in development?
faq.a: Most premature babies do catch up between ages 2–3 years. Very premature infants (born before 28 weeks) may take longer and might need specialized follow-up care.

faq.q: How do I read growth percentiles for my premature baby?
faq.a: Use **corrected age** to find your baby's percentile on WHO growth charts (up to age 2). For example, if your baby is 6 months chronological but 4 months corrected, compare to the 4-month percentiles.

faq.q: Do late premature babies (34–36 weeks) need corrected age?
faq.a: Yes, though the adjustment is smaller (4–6 weeks). It's recommended to use corrected age until at least age 1 for late premature babies.

faq.q: When do premature babies start walking?
faq.a: Premature babies typically walk between 12–18 months of **corrected age** (not chronological). A baby born at 28 weeks might walk around 15–18 months old chronologically, which is completely normal.

faq.q: How accurate is the corrected age calculator?
faq.a: The calculation is standard across pediatrics: corrected age = chronological age minus (40 weeks minus gestational weeks at birth). It's accurate and widely recommended by AAP and other pediatric organizations.

sources.name: AAP — Follow-up Care of High-Risk Infants
